description.en: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus causing breathing difficulty.

en.name: Ginger Tea
en.method: Slice fresh ginger. Boil in water for 10–15 minutes. Strain and add honey.
en.use: Consumed warm to help with cough and congestion.

en.name: Steam Inhalation
en.method: Pour hot water into a bowl. Carefully inhale steam for 5–10 minutes.
en.use: Used to loosen mucus and ease breathing discomfort.

en.name: Turmeric Milk
en.method: Heat milk. Add turmeric and pepper. Stir well.
en.use: Consumed warm for throat comfort and inflammation support.

en.name: Warm Soup / Broth
en.ingredients: Vegetables or chicken, Water, Mild spices
en.method: Boil ingredients until soft. Simmer well and serve warm.
en.use: Helps hydration and provides nutrition during illness.

description.en: Tuberculosis (TB) is a serious bacterial infection primarily affecting the lungs. Note: Medical treatment is essential; natural remedies are supportive only.

en.name: Garlic Water
en.method: Crush garlic cloves. Add to warm water. Let sit briefly before drinking.
en.use: Traditionally consumed for respiratory support.

en.name: Amla Juice
en.method: Blend amla with water. Strain the juice. Add honey if desired.
en.use: Consumed for vitamin C and immune support.

en.name: Mint Tea
en.method: Boil mint leaves in water. Simmer for several minutes. Strain before drinking.
en.use: Used for breathing comfort and freshness.

en.name: Protein-Rich Soup
en.method: Boil ingredients thoroughly. Simmer until soft. Serve warm.
en.use: Supports nutrition and recovery strength.

description.en: COVID-19 is a viral respiratory illness caused by SARS-CoV-2, affecting lungs and causing fever, dry cough, and fatigue.

en.name: Nilavembu Kudineer
en.method: Add 5–10 g powder to water. Boil until reduced by half. Filter and drink warm.
en.use: Widely consumed for immune and fever support.

en.name: Salt Water Gargle
en.method: Mix salt into warm water. Gargle for 20–30 seconds.
en.use: Used for sore throat relief.

en.name: Lemon Honey Drink
en.method: Add lemon juice to warm water. Mix honey well.
en.use: Consumed for throat comfort and hydration.

en.name: Turmeric Ginger Tea
en.method: Boil ginger and turmeric in water. Simmer for 10 minutes. Strain before drinking.
en.use: Traditionally consumed for warmth and respiratory comfort.

description.en: A bone fracture is a break or crack in a bone. Note: Fracture needs proper medical treatment (casting/surgery); remedies are supportive.

en.name: Calcium-Rich Milk
en.method: Warm the milk slightly. Add turmeric and mix well. Add honey if needed.
en.use: Supports bone healing by providing calcium and protein.

en.name: Sesame Seed Mix
en.method: Roast sesame seeds lightly. Mix with jaggery or honey. Eat in small portions.
en.use: Rich in calcium and supports bone strength.

en.name: Egg / Protein Soup
en.method: Boil ingredients well. Cook into soft soup. Serve warm.
en.use: Helps tissue and bone repair.

en.name: Turmeric Ginger Drink
en.method: Boil ginger and turmeric in water. Strain and drink warm.
en.use: Helps reduce inflammation and pain.

ayurveda.name: Laksha Guggulu
ayurveda.dosage: 2 tablets twice daily with warm milk

ayurveda.name: Asthisamharaka
ayurveda.dosage: 5g powder with warm milk, twice daily

description.en: Arthritis causes inflammation of joints resulting in pain, stiffness, and reduced mobility.

en.name: Turmeric Milk
en.method: Heat milk. Add turmeric and pepper. Stir well.
en.use: Helps reduce joint inflammation.

en.name: Ginger Tea
en.method: Boil ginger in water for 10–15 minutes. Strain and drink warm.
en.use: May reduce joint pain and stiffness.

en.name: Epsom Salt Bath
en.method: Add Epsom salt to warm water. Soak joints for 15–20 minutes.
en.use: Helps relax muscles and reduce stiffness.

en.name: Fenugreek Water
en.method: Soak seeds overnight. Boil or drink soaked water.
en.use: Traditionally used for joint pain relief.

ayurveda.name: Mahayogaraj Guggulu
ayurveda.dosage: 2 tablets twice daily with warm water

description.en: Active bacterial, viral, or fungal skin pathogen detected (indicative of cellulitis, impetigo, or acute dermatitis). The epidermal and dermal layers show acute localized vascular dilation (erythema) with dynamic inflammatory fluid infiltration. This causes high localized warmth, redness, and swelling as the body's white blood cells combat the pathogen. Prompt topical or systemic antimicrobial therapy is recommended to prevent spreading to deeper subcutaneous tissues.

en.name: Neem Wash
en.method: Boil neem leaves in water, cool and use to wash infected area.
en.use: Natural antiseptic wash.

en.name: Aloe Vera Gel
en.method: Apply fresh gel to affected area for cooling and healing.
en.use: Soothes inflammation and itching.

en.name: Turmeric Paste
en.method: Make a paste and apply to infection for 20 mins then wash.
en.use: Natural antibacterial treatment.

en.name: Warm Salt Compress
en.method: Dip cloth in salt water and apply to area to reduce swelling.
en.use: Helps draw out infection and pus.

description.en: Chronic autoimmune-mediated epidermal hyperplasia observed (highly consistent with Psoriasis Vulgaris or severe atopic Dermatitis). The visual texture signature indicates localized hyperkeratosis (silvery scaly buildup) combined with underlying capillary dilation. This condition involves accelerated skin cell turnover triggered by inflammatory cytokines. Maintaining skin barrier moisture is essential, and systemic or topical immune modulation should be evaluated by a dermatologist.

en.name: Coconut-Turmeric Mix
en.method: Mix and apply to affected areas.
en.use: Soothes itching and reduces scales.

en.name: Oatmeal Bath
en.method: Soak in a lukewarm bath with oatmeal for 15 minutes.
en.use: Relieves itching and softens scales.

en.name: Aloe Vera Application
en.method: Apply gel 3 times daily to scaly patches.
en.use: Reduces redness and scaling.

en.name: Apple Cider Vinegar
en.method: Dab diluted solution onto itchy patches then rinse.
en.use: Helps with scalp and skin itching.
